name: Percy Visual Testing API (BrowserStack)
what_it_does: Percy (now part of BrowserStack) API for visual testing and screenshot comparison. Enables AI agents to retrieve visual diff results, manage builds, access screenshot comparisons, and integrate visual regression data into CI/CD workflows. Percy captures UI snapshots and uses AI to detect meaningful visual changes vs noise.
use_cases: Retrieving visual diff results and UI regressions from QA automation agents
Managing Percy build status and approval workflows from CI/CD pipeline agents
Analyzing visual change history across browser environments from visual QA agents
Integrating screenshot comparison data into deployment decision agents
not_for: Functional or behavioral testing (Percy is visual-only)
best_when: A front-end team wants AI agents to catch visual regressions automatically — integrating Percy's diff data into review and deployment workflows.
avoid_when: You don't have significant UI complexity or design systems. Percy adds overhead for simple UIs.

auth.notes: Percy token-based authentication. PERCY_TOKEN environment variable for SDK integration. Project-scoped tokens.

pricing.paid_tiers: Team and Enterprise plans; pricing by screenshot volume
pricing.notes: Now part of BrowserStack. Generous free tier for small teams. Volume pricing for enterprise.

known_agent_gotchas: No public MCP server — REST API integration required
Percy builds are async — agents must poll build status before reading results
Visual diffs require human review approval — agents can surface but not auto-approve
Screenshot data includes sensitive application UI — handle carefully
BrowserStack integration may affect Percy API endpoint structure going forward
agent_readiness.security_notes: Screenshots capture full application UI including sensitive data. SOC2. Token auth. Treat PERCY_TOKEN as a secret.

security_score_components.security_notes: Visual screenshots contain sensitive UI. SOC2 via BrowserStack. Token-scoped access.
